claim: The existing system is 70-80% of the way to the clean-slate architecture ideal, with the three roles (GOVERN/KNOW/RECALL) already implemented as CLAUDE.md/MEMORY.md/JSONL state files.
The hybrid approach (D8a) and clean-slate design (D9a) are not competing answers — D9a is the design target and D8a is the incremental route to reach it.
Setting autoMemoryDirectory to point at .claude/canonical-memory/ (git-tracked) is the highest-signal single action for cross-locale sync, requiring zero new infrastructure.

The canonical-memory directory is currently diverged: missing approximately 7 feedback entries and incorrectly describing user expertise as 'Node.js expert' rather than 'non-developer director.' Reconciliation is required before autoMemoryDirectory is configured.

AutoDream is confirmed LIVE on this account (tengu_onyx_plover flag). Research findings that describe it as server-gated are superseded by user confirmation. Testing AutoDream behavior for 3-5 sessions before adding dream-skill is required to avoid conflict.

4 of 14 current mechanisms have drifted from their intended function, giving a ~29% base-rate probability that any new mechanism will be abandoned within 20 sessions unless it is zero-maintenance or fully automated.

The session-end pipeline is the load-bearing ritual: if it is skipped, SESSION_CONTEXT, TDMS consolidation, learning system updates, and proposed enhancements all fail. It must not be lengthened beyond what can be executed in 2-3 minutes.

The MCP memory startup hang risk (5-6 minutes, closed NOT_PLANNED by Anthropic) is permanent. The mitigation — never add 'check MCP memory at session start' to CLAUDE.md or any skill — must remain in place indefinitely.

Chroma (npm chromadb) is ARM64-only on Windows x64 and cannot be used as a vector store in this environment. This is documented in claude-mem Issue #1146 and applies to any tool using the npm chromadb package.

sqlite-vec is the best-fit vector store for this environment — embedded, no server process, installs via npm, v0.1.9 confirmed released 2026-03-31. Critical gap: npm package behavior on Windows x64 without admin has not been independently verified in this specific environment.
The existing mcp__memory knowledge graph is adequate for current scale (~500-1,500 entities). Enhancement: set MEMORY_FILE_PATH to a git-tracked path so the graph syncs cross-locale via git.
Neo4j is eliminated as a knowledge graph option — requires admin (Windows installer) or Docker (also admin). Hard block for this environment regardless of capability.

Local ONNX embeddings via @huggingface/transformers + all-MiniLM-L6-v2 are the correct primary embedding choice: zero cost, full privacy, offline, no admin. Known gap: Node.js v22 compatibility not independently verified.
Voyage AI is rejected despite Anthropic's endorsement: default retains training data, opt-out requires admin dashboard access, and cost is 3x OpenAI's backup option.

SQLite via cloud sync (OneDrive, Dropbox) carries silent corruption risk. SQLite official docs explicitly state network filesystem use is unsupported. Keep SQLite stores (L3 episodic layer) locale-local only.

The content-type-prior filter (A-MAC paper) eliminates approximately 70% of low-signal memory write candidates and is the single highest-leverage memory quality improvement available at near-zero cost.

Adding a Memory Admission Policy whitelist to CLAUDE.md is the highest ROI per hour of any pattern in the research — 45 minutes, zero infrastructure, immediate quality improvement on every future session.
The existing feedback_*.md file convention is already an implicit content-type-prior implementation. Making it explicit and extending it to all memory categories is formalization, not invention.

Confidence decay (TTL) must NEVER be applied to behavioral corrections (feedback_*.md) or architectural decisions. The failure mode — re-teaching corrected behaviors because they were decayed — is worse than the memory entropy the decay was meant to solve.

Restructuring MEMORY.md as a token-aware progressive disclosure index (adding ~tokens, type, salience columns) reduces unnecessary topic file loading and improves session startup efficiency. Takes 2-3 hours, zero new infrastructure.
Adding the episodic_memory_show permission to the allow-list is a 15-minute, zero-risk enhancement that completes the episodic retrieval path from search-only to search+retrieve.

codebase-memory-mcp fills a structural code intelligence gap that no session memory tool addresses. Windows amd64 binary, auto-detect install, near-zero ongoing maintenance. Complementary to session memory, not competing.

Engram (Go binary, FTS5, MCP stdio, Bare MCP mode) is the best standalone explicit knowledge store for this stack — aligns with the Go tool pattern already deployed, no shell dependencies in Bare MCP mode, export/import for cross-machine sync.

claude-mem must not be installed as a primary framework: 5 documented Windows-specific failures, CLAUDE.md subdirectory pollution bug closed NOT_PLANNED, AGPL-3.0 license, Bun+ChromaDB+PowerShell dependency chain the no-admin work locale cannot satisfy.

The autoMemoryDirectory setting was shipped in Claude Code v2.1.74 (March 12, 2026) specifically to support redirecting memory writes to git-tracked or cloud-synced paths. It is the official mechanism for cross-locale memory sync.
autoMemoryDirectory cannot be set in project settings (.claude/settings.json, git-tracked). It must be manually set in settings.local.json at each locale. This is a security restriction, not a bug.

The Supermemory cloud plugin ($19/month) carries a Windows stdin hang (Issue #25, open since Feb 2026) and transmits full session transcripts to a third party. Both are disqualifying for this environment.
OMEGA Memory's 95.4% LongMemEval performance is irrelevant because it requires WSL2, which requires admin access unavailable at the work locale. Hard block regardless of quality.

The existing .claude/state/consolidation.json + run-consolidation.js pattern is exactly the infrastructure needed for a memory consolidation pipeline. The gap is not 'build a new system' but 'generalize the existing pattern from PR reviews to all memory types.'

The existing MEMORY.md taxonomy (user_*, feedback_*, project_*, reference_*) maps directly to the A-MAC content-type-prior categories. The admission filter is already implicitly implemented; it needs to be made explicit and enforced.
Context rot from injecting too many irrelevant memories is a confirmed risk. Distractors compound non-linearly: a single stale memory entry actively degrades output quality, not just wastes tokens.

Current startup token budget (~12,300-12,600 tokens estimated) is within safe bounds. A startup budget guard in session-begin should warn at >18,000 tokens — the threshold where degradation becomes measurable.

The LongMemEval benchmark shows approximately 30% accuracy drop on memorizing information across sustained interactions even for commercial assistants. Conservative retrieval design (fewer, higher-confidence memories) is warranted.

Consolidation that runs in context-free mode (AutoDream's architecture) risks compressing nuanced conditional rules into flat rules. Archive originals; never delete source memories during consolidation.

The 'minimum viable custom' principle applies: build only what Anthropic demonstrably will not ship. AutoDream is live, native cross-device sync is in the roadmap. Building infrastructure Anthropic ships in 6 months is wasted effort.

With 25 hooks already running, any new PostToolUse hook doing non-trivial work must use fire-and-forget queue architecture. The 30-second hook timeout is a hard constraint. A performance budget for new hooks is required before adding any.

The clean-slate architecture's primary gaps versus the current system are: (a) no admission gate at write time, (b) no decay/TTL enforcement, (c) consolidation not generalized beyond PR reviews, (d) canonical-memory divergence. All four are addressable without replacing any existing mechanism.

Reasoning trace capture (storing how Claude reasoned, not just what it decided) is supported by Reflexion (NeurIPS 2023) showing +11% HumanEval improvement from re-reading past reasoning traces. A lightweight reasoning-traces.jsonl implements this without cipher's full infrastructure.

Memory promotion rules (project-scoped knowledge elevating to global scope only after 3+ session recurrences) would prevent project-specific noise from polluting global memory. The existing MEMORY.md taxonomy already has the scope structure; the rule formalizes it.

Phase-based rule loading via .claude/rules/ glob-scoped files can reduce CLAUDE.md effective token load from ~4,800 to ~3,500 tokens for sessions where phase-specific sections are irrelevant. The .claude/rules/ directory already supports this.
T2-D (completing autoMemoryDirectory at the home locale) requires physical access to the home machine and cannot be done remotely. It should be scheduled as a dedicated session at that locale.

The autoMemoryDirectory + git-tracked directory pattern appears to be novel — the research found no reference implementations of this specific combination. Treat Phase 1 as an experiment with a defined rollback path.

STATE_SCHEMA.md documents 10 state files while the actual state directory has 82 files — 8x drift. Fixing this is a T1 free win: one session, 20-30 minutes, prevents wasted orientation time in every future session.

Docker-based memory tools (Hindsight, OpenMemory/Mem0, Qdrant standalone, Weaviate) are eliminated for this environment regardless of quality ratings. Docker requires Hyper-V or WSL2; no admin at work locale is a hard block.
